title: Molten Nutty Moroccan Fish Harira
description: A pescatarian-friendly Moroccan stew featuring malt vinegar and nutty flavors, served en-croute, perfect for a monsoon evening. This dish is a twist on the traditional Harira, with a molten center and a blend of spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the sliced almonds and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
In a large bowl, whisk together the chicken broth, malt vinegar, paprika, and cumin.
Add the cod fillets to the bowl and marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Roll out the flour to a thickness of about 1/8 inch and cut into 4 equal pieces.
Place a piece of the cod in the center of each piece of flour, spoon some of the almond mixture on top, and brush the edges with the beaten egg.
Fold the flour over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the en-croute fish on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with the remaining egg wash.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the fish is cooked through.
